reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: There are extensive omissions in the output. Pages 1 through 12 of the source document—containing the Meeting Agenda, Meeting Minutes, and FCC Background/Fact Sheets—are entirely missing. The conversion abruptly begins at the end of paragraph 8 on page 13. Furthermore, although the headings for NPRM sections 'III.C Petition for Determination' and 'III.D Other Section 902 Provisions' are included in the JSON, the corresponding body text (source pages 20-21) is completely absent.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Entire content of pages 1-12; Body text for Section III.C (Paragraphs 30-31) and Section III.D (Paragraphs 32-33).
concerns.output_quote: The JSON output begins with the partial sentence 'to Congress on 911 fees].”' from page 13. Sections III.C and III.D contain empty element arrays.
concerns.location: Reading Order 1, Sections 'III.C Petition for Determination' (index 5) and 'III.D Other Section 902 Provisions' (index 6)

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Appendix B (Initial Regulatory Flexibility Analysis) has undergone extreme truncation. The JSON output only includes paragraphs 1, 2, 5, 6, 13, 15, and 19. Twelve key paragraphs (3, 4, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 14, 16, 17, and 18) containing the primary legal and regulatory analysis of the section are missing, which constitutes an omission of approximately 70% of the section's content.
concerns.output_quote: The list items in the section titled 'Initial Regulatory Flexibility Analysis' skip from paragraph 2 to 5, 6 to 13, etc.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is severely broken. Major sections 'C. Petition for Determination' and 'D. Other Section 902 Provisions' (Source pages 20-21) exist as empty section objects in the JSON. Their actual content has been incorrectly merged into an ordered list within section 'A. Definitions and Applicability'. Additionally, top-level sections like 'APPENDIX A' and 'APPENDIX B' are nested as level 3 headings inside the 'V. ORDERING CLAUSES' section instead of being treated as independent major s
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 20: C. Petition for Determination; Page 21: D. Other Section 902 Provisions; Page 25: APPENDIX A; Page 28: APPENDIX B
concerns.output_quote: Section 5: title: 'C. Petition for Determination', elements: []; Section 6: title: 'D. Other Section 902 Provisions', elements: []; Section 8: contains 'heading' level 3 for 'APPENDIX A' and 'APPENDIX B'.

concerns.analysis: Table structures are frequently corrupted. In § 9.24 (Source page 27), plain text paragraphs have been forced into a table structure. More critically, the highly complex tables in the KOMO Contract (Page 43) and KXLY Invoice (Page 45-46) have been destroyed. In the KOMO 'Contract Details' table, 11 separate headers are concatenated into a single string in the first header cell, and data columns are similarly merged. The line items table in the KXLY invoice is converted into a single block of tex
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 43: Contract table with columns: Line, Ch, Start Date, End Date, Description, Start/End Time, Days, Length, Week, Rate, Type, Spots, Amount. Page 27: § 9.24 text.
concerns.output_quote: Section 10: Table 'Contract Details' header cell 1: '*Line Ch Start Date End Date Description Start/End Time Days Length Week Rate Type Spots Amount'. Section 10: Tables '§ 9.24 Petition rules part 1' and 'part 2'.
concerns.location: Section 10, Table 'Contract Details' and Section 10, § 9.24 tables

concerns.analysis: The reading order is fundamentally flawed. The document starts at page 13 of the source (the FCC doc), completely omitting the first 12 pages (Agenda and Minutes). These missing pages are only referenced by a placeholder at the very end of the document. Within the FCC document, content from major sections C and D (pages 20-21) has been moved and interleaved into a list in section A (page 13).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 1-12 contain the Agenda and Minutes. Pages 20-21 contain sections C and D.
concerns.output_quote: Output starts with text from page 13. Section 15: '[Pages 1-13 unavailable]'. Section 3: contains content from sections C and D as list items 5 and 7.
concerns.location: Start of document, Section 15, and Section 3

concerns.analysis: The output incorrectly converts several pages of distinct text paragraphs and sub-sections into a single massive ordered list. In section 'A. Definitions and Applicability', the pipeline has interpreted paragraph numbering (11, 12, 13, etc.) as list markers, flattening the entire structural flow of the discussion into a single list hierarchy that even absorbs separate section headings (C and D).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 13-24: Numbered paragraphs 11 through 33 and major sub-headings A, B, C, D.
concerns.output_quote: Section 3: A single 'list' type element containing nearly all content from the FCC document discussion, including items which should be separate paragraphs or sub-sections.

concerns.analysis: Multiple meaningful images and diagrams from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. These include the 'Major NG9-1-1 Program Areas' diagram on page 37, the 'NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'Call Handling Solution Architecture' diagrams on page 38, the 'WA 911 Jobs' campaign banner on page 40, and both the line chart and photograph in the 'Analytics' and 'The Commercial' sections on page 41. These graphics contain core informational content that is not present in the rendered HTML.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source PDF pages 37, 38, 40, and 41 contain meaningful visual content: a 'Major NG9-1-1 Program Areas' diagram (pg 37), 'NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'Call Handling Solution Architecture' diagrams (pg 38), a 'WA 911 Jobs' banner (pg 40), and an 'Analytics' line chart and 'The Commercial' photograph (pg 41).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML rendered output contains the headers for these sections but lacks all corresponding images and diagrams, leaving the sections visually incomplete.
concerns.location: NG9-1-1 Program Areas section (pg 37), NC 911 Board section (pg 38), WA 911 Jobs section (pg 40), Analytics and The Commercial sections (pg 41)

concerns.description: Significant word count loss: 51% of baseline
